>

-module(solution).
-export([solve/1]).

%% solve(nums) -> list_of_effective_elements
solve(Nums) ->
    case Nums of
        [] -> []
        [H] -> [H]
        _ ->
            N = length(Nums),
            ValidIndices = find_valid_indices(Nums, N),
            [Nums !! I || I <- ValidIndices].

%% Helper function to find all valid indices
find_valid_indices(Nums, N) ->
    % The first element (index 0) and the last element (index N-1) are always valid
    [0 | ValidMiddle] ++ [N-1],
    where
        -- Iterate from the second element (index 1) up to the second-to-last element (index N-2)
        ValidMiddle =
            lists:filter(is_valid_middle_element(Nums), lists:seq(1, N-2)).

%% Helper predicate to check if an element at index I is valid
is_valid_middle_element(Nums, I) ->
    % An element Nums[I] is valid if:
    % 1. It is strictly greater than all elements to its left (Nums[0]...Nums[I-1])
    % OR
    % 2. It is strictly greater than all elements to its right (Nums[I+1]...Nums[N-1])
    
    Left = lists:take(I, Nums),
    Right = lists:drop(I+1, Nums),
    
    is_greater_than_all(Nums !! I, Left)
    or
    is_greater_than_all(Nums !! I, Right).

%% Helper predicate to check if a value is strictly greater than all elements in a list
is_greater_than_all(Value, List) ->
    case List of
        [] -> true; % Trivially true if the list is empty
        _ -> 
            lists:all(fun(X) -> Value > X end, List)
    end.

-module_private.

%% @doc
%% Given an integer array nums, return an array containing all valid elements.
%% An element nums[i] is valid if it is strictly greater than all elements to its left,
%% OR strictly greater than all elements to its right.
%% The first and last elements are always valid.
%% @spec solve([integer()]) -> [integer()].
solve(Nums) ->
    case Nums of
        [] -> []
        [H] -> [H]
        _ ->
            % Precompute LMax: LMax[i] stores the maximum element in nums[0...i-1]
            LMax = compute_lmax(Nums),
            % Precompute RMax: RMax[i] stores the maximum element in nums[i+1...N-1]
            RMax = compute_rmax(Nums),
            % Check validity for each element
            ValidNums = lists:foldl(check_element(LMax, RMax), [], Nums, 0),
            ValidNums
end.

%% Helper to compute LMax array
%% LMax[i] = max(Nums[0]...Nums[i-1])
compute_lmax(Nums) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) -> lists:append(Acc, [max_so_far]) end, [], Nums, 0).
% Since Erlang lists are not optimized for random access, we use a functional approach
% where the result list represents the running maximums needed for the left side check.
% However, the structure required here is more complex than a simple fold because we need the max *before* i.

% A simpler approach using recursion or list processing to build the prefix/suffix max arrays.

% Let's use a helper that generates the required maximums lists.

compute_lmax_list(Nums) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) ->
        case Acc of
            [] -> [X]
            [LastMax | Rest] -> [max(LastMax, X) | Rest]
        end
    end, [], Nums).

% This is tricky due to the required structure. Let's re-evaluate the need for full arrays.
% We only need to know if nums[i] > max(nums[0...i-1]) AND nums[i] > max(nums[i+1...N-1]).

% Let N be the length of Nums.
% L[i] = max(Nums[0]...Nums[i-1]) (L[0] is effectively -infinity)
% R[i] = max(Nums[i+1]...Nums[N-1]) (R[N-1] is effectively -infinity)

% Function to calculate prefix maximums (L array)
calculate_prefix_max(Nums) ->
    PrefixMaxes = [0 | lists:map(fun(X) -> X end, Nums)], % Placeholder: [0] represents -inf
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) ->
        case Acc of
            [] -> [X]
            [LastMax | Rest] ->
                NewMax = max(LastMax, X),
                [NewMax | Rest]
        end
    end, [0], Nums).

% Due to Erlang's immutability and list structure, generating an array/list where index i
% depends on elements before i requires careful list construction.

% Let's stick to the definition:
% LMax[i] = max(Nums[0]...Nums[i-1])
% RMax[i] = max(Nums[i+1]...Nums[N-1])

% Helper function to generate the LMax list (size N)
generate_lmax(Nums) ->
    generate_lmax_recursive(Nums, 0, []).

generate_lmax_recursive([], _, Acc) -> lists:reverse(Acc).
generate_lmax_recursive([H|T], Index, Acc) ->
    case Acc of
        [] -> % First element (i=0), LMax[0] is -infinity (or smaller than any possible element)
              % Since elements >= 1, we use 0.
            LMax_i = 0,
            NewAcc = [LMax_i | Acc]
        [PrevMax|_] -> % LMax[i] = max(LMax[i-1], Nums[i-1])

        end

    ; Num > LMax[i] -> true
    % Condition 2: Strictly greater than right side maximum

        Element = lists:nth(I, Nums),
        
        % Check condition 1: strictly greater than all elements to the left
        LeftMax = case lists:sublist(Nums, 1, I - 1) of
                    [] -> -1; % Should not happen if I > 1
                    List -> lists:max(List)
                end,
        Condition1 = Element > LeftMax,

        % Check condition 2: strictly greater than all elements to the right
        RightMax = case lists:sublist(Nums, I + 1, Length - I) of
                    [] -> -1; % Should not happen if I < Length
                    List -> lists:max(List)
                end,
        Condition2 = Element > RightMax,

        Condition1; % At least one condition must be true, but since we check the boundary cases first, 
                     % for intermediate elements, it must satisfy (C1 OR C2). 
                     % However, the problem statement says:
                     % "If element nums[i] satisfies AT LEAST ONE of the conditions..."
                     % Wait, the example suggests it must satisfy EITHER C1 OR C2.
                     % Let's re-read the definition:
                     % 1. Strictly greater than its left side.
                     % 2. Strictly greater than its right side.
                     % If EITHER is true, it's valid.
        Condition1 or Condition2
    end.

%% Helper to build the result list from valid indices
EnumList(Nums, Indices) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, I) -> [lists:nth(I+1, Nums) | Acc] end, [], lists:reverse(Indices)).
